note: Meaning-closure over the chaos->definition edge: each intent is a falsifiable statement, bound to a gate + negative control, ratified by the operator. Verified by tools/intent_contract.py --check.

INTENT-GATE-SOUNDNESS.statement: Every meta-verification gate proves it is falsifiable (a negative control resolving to a defined test); the legacy gate surface is frozen debt, never silently absorbed.

INTENT-FAIL-CLOSED.statement: Gate code fails closed: an except-handler that swallows an error into success is detected across tools and src.

INTENT-CLAIM-COVERAGE.statement: The claim-evidence graph is complete: no dangling, missing-evidence, orphan, or unbacked asserted claim.

INTENT-DETERMINISM.statement: Committed generated artifacts are byte-reproducible AND fresh: nondeterministic or stale-drift artifacts fail closed.

INTENT-COMPLEXITY.statement: No function exceeds cyclomatic complexity 15 (ratchet), and stale allowlist entries expire rather than rot the ratchet.

INTENT-SSRF-SAFE.statement: External ingest is SSRF-safe: only https AND allowlisted arXiv hosts may be fetched; XML is parsed defused.

INTENT-DASHBOARD-HONEST.statement: The quality dashboard is a computed composite that cannot fabricate a PASS: a mypy usage error is fail-closed, and any failing dimension propagates.

INTENT-MEANING-CLOSURE.statement: Every asserted verification intent is falsifiable, ratified, and bound to a resolving negative control; an unratified or unbound intent fails closed.
